items.title: Meta releases Llama 4, a new crop of flagship AI models | TechCrunch
items.author: techcrunch.com
items.snippet: Meta's most significant recent open source AI release is the Llama 4 model family, announced in April 2025. The Llama 4 herd introduces two flagship models: Llama 4 Scout (17B active parameters, 109B total, with 16 experts and a massive 10-million-token context window) and Llama 4 Maverick (17B active parameters, 400B total, with 128 experts). These are Meta's first natively multimodal open-weight models built using a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture, pre-trained on 200 languages and design

items.title: GitHub Copilot coding agent 101: Getting started with agentic workflows on GitHub - The GitHub Blog
items.author: github.blog
items.snippet: GitHub has made a series of major AI agent announcements for GitHub Copilot over 2025–2026. In February 2025, GitHub introduced agent mode for Copilot in VS Code, enabling it to autonomously iterate on code, recognize and fix errors, suggest terminal commands, and perform self-healing at runtime — marking a shift from assistant to autonomous agent. Around the same time, GitHub previewed 'Project Padawan,' a codename for Copilot's autonomous SWE agent capable of generating/reviewing code, refacto

items.title: What's New in Microsoft 365 Copilot | January 2026 | Microsoft Community Hub
items.author: techcommunity.microsoft.com
items.snippet: Microsoft has been actively expanding its Copilot AI capabilities throughout early 2026, with a strong focus on agentic, multi-step task execution across its product suite. Key announcements include the general availability of Agent 365 (at $15/user starting May 1), 'Copilot Cowork' for long-running collaborative tasks, and Wave 3 of Microsoft 365 Copilot which introduces next-generation agentic experiences embedded directly in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook. Microsoft also announced a new
